## [00:00 - 01:16]

It's really important early in our lives to define the difference between confidence and arrogance. I believe confidence is people who believe that others are equal to them. I'm not better than you, but also you're not better than me. We're on the same playing field and when confident people walk through life, thinking in this way, they're more open to opportunities, they're more open to learning from other people. Therefore, I believe, become more successful. Whereas arrogant people, they go through life believing that they are better than others, that they're on a whole other level and you're on a different playing field, they're on a different playing field. And because they believe this, they go through life, closing off their mind, closing off their heart, not open to new opportunities and as a result they don't learn from other people. And as a result, I find arrogant people tend to not succeed. Actually, I'll take that statement back. Sometimes they do succeed, but when they do, everyone around them hates them anyway. A great example of that is Craig, the man right behind this camera, arrogant bastard, that guy. Make me sick. So, just after talking about Craig, it leaves a bad taste in my mouth.
